sn35.230:1.1: “Monks, just as if a fisherman were to cast a baited hook into a deep lake
sn35.230:1.2: and a fish with its eye out for food would swallow it—
sn35.230:1.3: so that the fish that had thus swallowed the fisherman’s hook would fall into misfortune & disaster, and the fisherman could do with it as he will—
sn35.230:2.1: in the same way, there are these six hooks in the world for the misfortune of beings, for the slaughter of those that breathe.
sn35.230:2.3: “There are forms, monks, cognizable via the eye—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked to sensual desire.
sn35.230:2.4: If a monk relishes them, welcomes them, & remains fastened to them,
sn35.230:2.5: he is said to be a monk who has swallowed Māra’s hook, who has fallen into misfortune & disaster. The Evil One can do with him as he will. “There are sounds cognizable via the ear… “There are aromas cognizable via the nose…
sn35.230:2.6: “There are flavors cognizable via the tongue…
sn35.230:3.1: “There are tactile sensations cognizable via the body… “There are ideas cognizable via the intellect—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked to sensual desire.
sn35.230:3.2: If a monk relishes them, welcomes them, & remains fastened to them,
sn35.230:3.3: he is said to be a monk who has swallowed Māra’s hook, who has fallen into misfortune & disaster. The Evil One can do with him as he will.
sn35.230:4.1: “Now, there are forms cognizable via the eye—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked to sensual desire.
sn35.230:4.2: If a monk does not relish them, welcome them, or remain fastened to them, he is said to be a monk who has not swallowed Māra’s hook, who has snapped the hook, who has broken the hook, who has not fallen into misfortune & disaster. The Evil One cannot do with him as he will. “There are sounds cognizable via the ear… “There are aromas cognizable via the nose…
sn35.230:5.1: “There are flavors cognizable via the tongue…
sn35.230:5.2: “There are tactile sensations cognizable via the body… “There are ideas cognizable via the intellect—agreeable, pleasing, charming, endearing, enticing, linked to sensual desire.
sn35.230:5.3: If a monk does not relish them, welcome them, or remain fastened to them, he is said to be a monk who has not swallowed Māra’s hook, who has snapped the hook, who has broken the hook, who has not fallen into misfortune & disaster. The Evil One cannot do with him as he will.”
